finding: A simple backlog/audit request required a multi-hop `next -> guide -> orient -> start` sequence, and `orient --json` embedded the repository's full testEntrypoints list instead of a compact capability summary. This creates avoidable latency and large tool output before any useful work begins.
expectedBehavior: Read-only orientation and simple governance-document workflows should default to compact output: language, package manager, governance state, blockers, and a small validator summary. Full validator inventory should be opt-in or referenced by digest/path.
evidenceOrRepro: The 2026-07-19 `orient --json` response for this request emitted well over 100 declared test entrypoints, including nearly every `validate:*` script, although the requested work only needed backlog shards and plan evidence. The preceding guide route already knew README was read and no atom mutation was requested.
followUp: Add `--compact` as the default AI-facing orientation projection, move full test inventory behind `--full`, and allow `next` to invoke the exact backlog/review playbook without requiring a guidance session start. Instrument route hop count, output bytes, and time-to-first-action.
